channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NASCAR superstar Kyle Busch dies at 41 after hospitalization with 'severe illness'
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won more races than anyone across NASCAR's three national series, has died at 41. A joint statement from the Busch Family, Richard Childress Racing and NASCAR said he died after being hospitalized.
item.content:encoded:
Kyle Busch, who won more races than anyone across NASCAR's three national series, has died at 41. A joint statement from the Busch Family, Richard Childress Racing and NASCAR said he died after being hospitalized.

(Image credit: Derik Hamilton)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's towering arch clears another federal hurdle, despite public pushback
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts gave the arch design its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components. The president said Thursday that he doesn't need approval from Congress.
item.content:encoded:
The Commission of Fine Arts gave the arch design its final approval, even though it's missing some visual components. The president said Thursday that he doesn't need approval from Congress.

(Image credit: Rachel Treisman)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Republican congressman explains his objections to $1.8B Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., about his concerns with the so-called Anti-Weaponization Fund.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Congressman Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., about his concerns with the so-called Anti-Weaponization Fund.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Handel

item.title: American passenger feels 'betrayed' by federal order to stay in hantavirus quarantine
item.description: Two passengers from the cruise ship that had a hantavirus outbreak were blocked by the federal government from leaving the quarantine unit. One says she is being detained against her will.
item.content:encoded:
Two passengers from the cruise ship that had a hantavirus outbreak were blocked by the federal government from leaving the quarantine unit. One says she is being detained against her will.

(Image credit: Dylan Widger)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Senate Republicans rebel against Trump's $1.8 trillion fund
item.description: In a rare rebuke, Senate Republicans postponed a vote to fund immigration enforcement amid alarm over the novel fund
item.content:encoded:
In a rare rebuke, Senate Republicans postponed a vote to fund immigration enforcement amid alarm over the novel fund

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Palestinians drop bid for a senior U.N. role after U.S. pressure
item.description: The Palestinian delegation to the United Nations has dropped its bid to secure a top U.N. job after the U.S. threatens to revoke their visas.
item.content:encoded:
The Palestinian delegation to the United Nations has dropped its bid to secure a top U.N. job after the U.S. threatens to revoke their visas.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Democrats wanted answers for what went wrong in 2024. Now, there are more questions
item.description: The Democratic Party wanted a review of the 2024 election. Then, the DNC learned the report was incomplete and unverifiable, party chairman Ken Martin said Thursday, releasing an annotated version.
item.content:encoded:
The Democratic Party wanted a review of the 2024 election. Then, the DNC learned the report was incomplete and unverifiable, party chairman Ken Martin said Thursday, releasing an annotated version.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Abortion pills "just in case"? Planned Parenthood will offer them in two states
item.description: In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.
item.content:encoded:
In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Forecasters expect slightly fewer hurricanes than usual this year, but the risk of destructive storms is still high
item.description: Forecasters expect 8 to 14 storms will form in the Atlantic between June 1 and November 30. But the danger is more serious than the numbers suggest.
item.content:encoded:
Forecasters expect 8 to 14 storms will form in the Atlantic between June 1 and November 30. But the danger is more serious than the numbers suggest.

(Image credit: Matias Delacroix)
item.dc:creator: Jenny Staletovich

item.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
item.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

(Image credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez)
item.dc:creator: Diego Ibarra Sánchez
